title: G1: Ground the team efficacy -> performance mechanism in Social Cognitive Theory

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: Establish, with explicit justification (Bandura 1997, Social Cognitive Theory; collective efficacy), the motivational mechanism by which shared team efficacy raises team performance: higher collective efficacy => higher adopted goal levels, greater effort, persistence under obstacles, and resilience to failure. Derive from theory rather than asserting. Use the problem's definition exactly: team efficacy = the SHARED belief among members in the team's collective capability to perform tasks successfully (referent-shift consensus construct).
